questions.q: What is the main job of the executive branch?
questions.explanation: The executive branch executes the laws Congress passes.

questions.q: Besides the President, what makes up the executive branch?
options: The Vice President, Cabinet, departments, agencies, military, and federal workers
questions.explanation: It is a large organization of departments and agencies.

questions.q: Which agency handles citizenship and naturalization?
questions.explanation: USCIS handles naturalization and green cards.

questions.q: What are the detailed rules agencies write to carry out laws called?
questions.explanation: Agencies issue regulations within the authority Congress grants.

options: Career civil servants who serve across administrations
questions.explanation: The civil service provides continuity regardless of which party governs.
